title: Bridge Essay: Higher theta and alpha1 coherence when listening to Vedic recitation compared to

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nondual/Unity** (Phase 16) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Nondual/Unity to mechanistic neuroscience through Higher, theta, and, alpha1, coherence.

**Higher theta and alpha1 coherence when listening to Vedic recitation compared to coherence during Transcendental Meditation practice.** — Conscious Cogn. PMID: 28214765. DOI: 10.1016/j.concog.2017.02.002. This study compared subjective experiences and EEG patterns in 37 subjects when listening to live Vedic recitation and when practicing Transcendental Meditation (TM). Content analysis of experiences when listening to Vedic recitation yielded three higher-order code. Experiences during Vedic recitation were: (1) deeper than during TM practice; (2) experienced as an inner process; and (3) characterized by lively silence.
